questions.question: A planet's mass increases by $15%$. If its original mass was $4000$ kg, what is its new mass?
What is the percentage decrease from $50$ to $45$?
A lab experiment requires a $20%$ solution of a chemical. If you have $500$ ml of the chemical, how much water do you need to add?
A spaceship travels at $80%$ of its maximum speed. If its maximum speed is $1000$ km/h, how fast is it traveling?
What is the percentage increase from $30$ to $36$?
A space station's population decreases by $8%$. If its original population was $1200$, what is its new population?
A lab experiment requires a $30%$ increase in temperature. If the original temperature was $25^circ$C, what is the new temperature? Show your work and explain your reasoning.
A spaceship's fuel tank can hold $5000$ liters of fuel. If it is currently $20%$ full, how many more liters of fuel can be added? Show your work and explain your reasoning.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Encourage students to use real-world examples when solving percent problems.
Tip 2: Emphasize the importance of converting between percentages and decimals.
Tip 3: Remind students to check their units when solving problems involving percentages.
